channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇵🇱🇪🇺 Poland's Sikorski proposes EU-funded rapid reaction force for regional crises
item.description: Poland's foreign minister calls for a European rapid reaction force funded by the EU to handle neighborhood security operations, arguing Europe must become a more credible ally amid growing geopolitical risks.

item.title: 🇱🇹🇷🇺⚔️ Lithuania charges 13 people with attempted murders linked to Russian GRU
item.description: Lithuania has charged 13 individuals from multiple countries with two attempted murders in Vilnius connected to Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, marking a significant escalation in allegations of Russian intelligence operations on NATO territory.

item.title: 🇰🇵🇷🇺☢️ North Korea accelerates nuclear program with Russian support as Iran war erodes global security
item.description: Senior officials from Moscow and Pyongyang signal plans for long-term cooperation as North Korea capitalizes on the distraction of the Iran war to advance its nuclear ambitions, raising concerns about eroding global non-proliferation norms.

item.title: 🤖🏛️ Google employees petition CEO to refuse classified AI work with Pentagon
item.description: Hundreds of Google AI researchers have signed a letter urging CEO Sundar Pichai to refuse classified military AI work, reigniting debates about tech company involvement in defense projects.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇮🇱🌾 Ukraine warns Israel of diplomatic crisis over ship carrying alleged stolen grain
item.description: Ukraine has warned of a potential diplomatic and legal crisis with Israel if the vessel Panormitis, carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukraine, docks at Haifa port.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Mortar Attacks Kill Four in Afghanistan, Taliban Says
item.description: Mortar and rocket attacks launched by Pakistan against Afghanistan killed at least four people and injured 70 more, according to the Taliban government. The strikes mark another escalation in cross-border tensions between the neighbors.

item.title: 🇹🇩 Water Dispute Kills 42 in Eastern Chad as Conflict Escalates
item.description: At least 42 people were killed in eastern Chad after a dispute between two families over a water well escalated into a cycle of reprisal attacks. The government deployed the military to contain the violence as resource conflicts intensify.

item.title: ✈️ South Sudan Plane Crash Kills All 14 On Board Near Juba
item.description: A Cessna aircraft crashed southwest of South Sudan's capital Juba, killing all 14 people on board. The country's civil aviation authority said the crash occurred during adverse weather conditions.

item.title: 🇨🇴 Colombia Offers Record $1.4M Reward for Rebel Leader Behind Deadly Bombing
item.description: Colombia's government announced a record reward for information leading to the capture of a rebel leader known as "Marlon," accused of orchestrating a bus bombing that killed 20 people. The attack marks escalating pre-election violence.

item.title: 🤖 OpenAI Ends Microsoft Exclusivity, Opens Path to Amazon and Google Cloud Deals
item.description: Microsoft loses exclusive access to OpenAI technology, allowing the ChatGPT creator to sell products across rival cloud platforms in a major shift for the AI industry.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israeli Strikes Hit East Lebanon, Expanding Conflict Zone Despite Ceasefire
item.description: The Israeli military begins strikes in eastern Lebanon, expanding the scope of its bombing campaign during a ceasefire that has failed to halt hostilities with Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸🛢️ Iran Oil Tankers Forced Back by US Blockade as Hormuz Traffic Dwindles
item.description: Six tankers loaded with Iranian oil were forced to return to Iran by the US naval blockade in recent days, with ship-tracking data showing only minimal traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇮🇷 Putin pledges support for Iran, offers mediation in talks with Araghchi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin told Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg that Russia would support Iran through its "difficult period" and hoped for peace soon. The meeting comes as US-Iran talks stall.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇳🇴 Ukraine and Norway to jointly produce mid-strike drones
item.description: Ukraine and Norway will launch joint production of mid-range strike drones following a defense declaration signed in Oslo. The project expands Ukraine's drone manufacturing capabilities amid the ongoing war.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸🇮🇷 German Chancellor Merz Says Iran Humiliating US in Stalled Peace Talks
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the lack of progress in US-Iran negotiations, saying Tehran is making American officials travel to Pakistan and leave without results while the Iranian leadership continues to dictate terms.

item.title: 💰🌍📉 Iran War Creates Economic Strain Across Emerging Markets After Two Months
item.description: Two months since the outbreak of the Iran war, economic stress is spreading beyond the Middle East as emerging and developing markets face rising inflation, growing fiscal pressures, and widespread trade disruptions.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Suspends Steel Exports Through May Amid War-Related Strikes
item.description: Iran has banned exports of steel slabs and sheets until May 30, as the country's steel industry faces ongoing strikes during the conflict with Israel and the United States.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Mediators Continue Push to Bridge US-Iran Gaps After Failed Face-to-Face Talks
item.description: Pakistan-based mediators are still working to bridge gaps between the US and Iran despite the failure of direct diplomacy after Trump called off his envoys' trip over the weekend.

item.title: 🏛️ Record Government Debt to Persist as Military Spending Rises Globally
item.description: With government debt at historic highs across developed nations and rising military spending needs, fiscal watchdogs are being warned that calls for fiscal discipline will likely go unheeded.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇵🇸 Israel Withholds Palestinian Clearance Funds, Deducts $200 Million
item.description: Israel has decided to withhold clearance funds to the Palestinian Authority and deducted approximately $200 million this month, amid ongoing tensions over governance and security in the occupied territories.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇮🇷 Germany's Merz Says US Has 'No Exit Strategy' in Iran War
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized the US for lacking an exit strategy in the Iran conflict, calling Tehran a "skilled negotiator" as diplomatic efforts remain stalled.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇺🇦🚁 Ukraine to Help Poland Build Modern Drone Fleet Against Aerial Threats
item.description: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk announced Ukraine will assist Poland in building a "modern drone armada," citing Kyivs proven expertise in defending against aerial attacks.

item.title: 🇵🇸🏗️ Palestinians Use Gaza Rubble to Restore Streets as US Rebuilding Plan Stalls
item.description: Palestinians are crushing concrete and reusing war rubble to repave streets destroyed during Israels two-year assault on Gaza, as US-backed reconstruction efforts remain stalled.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇷🇺 Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Attacks on Politicians
item.description: German government suspects Russia is behind phishing attacks on Signal accounts targeting high-ranking politicians, including two ministers, military personnel and journalists. Around 300 accounts in the political sphere were compromised through fake security chatbot messages.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇺🇸 China Expands Economic Toolkit During Trade Truce With US
item.description: China has used its trade truce with the United States to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain and critical technology controls, and expand its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a summit between the nations next month.
